body.value: ○鹿児島藩 (書ナラス)
鹿児島藩。書ナラス (かきならず) — 「記載なし」「書面に出さない」の意。

Kagoshima domain — with the explicit annotation 書ナラス ('not written / not specified'). This is the most pedagogically significant annotation in HI: it literally documents Satsuma's refusal to disclose its military structure to the new central government. Satsuma at this point was the dominant Restoration power but politically still acting semi-independently.
○鹿児島藩
隊士 四百六十四人
隊士 (たいし) 四百六十四人
Taishi (corps-member) headcount = 464 persons. Printed at the head of Satsuma's text block on this page, paralleling the same figure on the kokudaka page.
464 persons
隊士
(一二) 隊長司令 / 士等役員 千百六人
(一・二) 隊長司令 / 士等役員 (したいやくいん) 千百六人

(1st/2nd) battalion commanders + shi-tō yakuin (officer-rank samurai-class members) = 1,106 persons. Distinct from the bare standing army count — these are officer-equivalent samurai with formal positions in the post-1869 reorganization.
1,106 persons
士等役員
兵卒人員 千五百六十八人
兵卒人員 (へいそつじんいん) 千五百六十八人 — 「庚午三月調」(明治三年三月)

Heisotsu jin'in (rank-and-file standing army) = 1,568 men, surveyed March 1870. This figure is dramatically below reality — Satsuma at this time fielded roughly 16,000 Spencer-rifle-armed troops (per Ravina's biography of Saigō, the 1868–1869 Boshin force, and Esposito's Osprey volume on the Boshin War). The 1,568 likely represents only the formal post-1869-reform standing army on paper, not the actual armed force.
1,568 persons
兵卒人員
大參事 一員 / 權大參事 九員 / 少參事 廿一員 / 權少參事 十六員 — (原來) 庚午三月調
大参事 (1) / 権大参事 (9) / 少参事 (21) / 権少参事 (16) の役職員数 — 庚午年三月(1870年3月)時点

Officer roster (March 1870 survey): Daisanji 1, Gondaisanji 9, Shōsanji 21, Gonshōsanji 16. These are the post-1869 hanseki-hōkan administrative titles after the daimyō formally returned the fief to the Emperor. Counting officers (but not armed strength) was Satsuma's pattern of partial disclosure.
1 + 9 + 21 + 16 officers
大参事 / 権大参事 / 少参事 / 権少参事
